_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to discuss how texts with the same purpose can be created differently for different audiences.

_source.w: We are learning to discuss how texts with the same purpose can be created differently for different audiences.
y: Understanding audience helps us create texts that speak directly to the people we are writing for.
Recognising how audience shapes a text helps us be more critical readers.
This skill is essential for all communication — from writing to speaking to creating.
eg: We compare an information text about animals written for young children with one written for older students.
We discuss how a persuasive text about recycling might change depending on whether it is for kids or adults.
We identify the audience of different texts and explain the clues that told us.
We rewrite a paragraph for a different audience and discuss what we changed.
We look at two versions of a recipe — one for beginners and one for experienced cooks — and compare them.
We create two versions of the same text — one for our peers and one for younger students.
